_note: SAMPLE / DEMO DATA — the four post-service tracks taught in transition programs. Not official DoD/VA guidance.

tracks.label: Employment
tracks.blurb: Go straight into a civilian career — leverage your experience, clearance, and veteran hiring programs.
fastFacts: DOL VETS reps and American Job Centers offer free one-on-one help.
DoD SkillBridge lets you intern with a civilian employer during your last 180 days — on military pay.
Federal jobs add veterans' preference points to your application.

tracks.label: Further education
tracks.blurb: Use the GI Bill or VR&E for a degree that opens the next career — with housing allowance while you study.
fastFacts: Post-9/11 GI Bill can cover tuition plus a monthly housing allowance.
Your military training may already count as college credit (see Smart transcript).
Student Veterans of America chapters make campuses easier to land on.

tracks.label: Skilled trades
tracks.blurb: Earn while you learn — apprenticeships and certifications into high-demand hands-on careers.
fastFacts: The GI Bill pays a housing stipend DURING registered apprenticeships — you earn a wage and the stipend.
Helmets to Hardhats connects veterans straight into building-trade apprenticeships.
Many military maintenance ratings shortcut FAA and trade certifications.

tracks.label: Entrepreneurship
tracks.blurb: Build your own mission — from a service business to federal contracting as a veteran-owned company.
fastFacts: SBA Boots to Business is a free two-day starter course for the military community.
Veterans Business Outreach Centers (VBOC) coach you through the plan for free.
SDVOSB certification opens set-aside federal contracts for service-disabled veteran owners.
